2603.21152.title: TRACE: A Multi-Agent System for Autonomous Physical Reasoning in Seismological
2603.21152.paper_summary: Inferring the physical mechanisms that govern earthquake sequences from indirect geophysical observations remains difficult, particularly across tectonically distinct environments where similar seismic patterns can reflect different underlying processes. Current interpretations rely heavily on the expert synthesis of catalogs, spatiotemporal statistics, and candidate physical models, limiting reproducibility and the systematic transfer of insight across settings. Here we present TRACE (Trans-perspective Reasoning and Automated Comprehensive Evaluator), a multi-agent system that combines large language model planning with formal seismological constraints to derive auditable, physically grounded mechanistic inference from raw observations. Applied to the 2019 Ridgecrest sequence, TRACE autonomously identifies stress-perturbation-induced delayed triggering, resolving the cascading interaction between the Mw 6.4 and Mw 7.1 mainshocks; in the Santorini-Kolumbo case, the system identifies a structurally guided intrusion model, distinguishing fault-channeled episodic migration from the continuous propagation expected in homogeneous crustal failure. By providing a generalizable logical infrastructure for interpreting heterogeneous seismic phenomena, TRACE advances the field from expert-dependent analysis toward knowledge-guided autonomous discovery in Earth sciences.
2603.21152.paper_summary_zh: 推斷控制地震序列的物理機制仍然困難，特別是在構造上截然不同的環境中，類似的地震模式可能反映不同的基礎過程。當前的解釋在很大程度上依賴於專家的綜合分析，包括目錄、時空統計和候選物理模型，這限制了可重複性以及在不同情境中系統性地轉移洞見。在此，我們提出TRACE（跨視角推理與自動綜合評估器），這是一個多代理系統，結合了大型語言模型規劃與正式的地震學約束，從原始觀測中推導出可審計的、基於物理的機制推斷。應用於2019年Ridgecrest序列，TRACE自動識別由應力擾動引起的延遲觸發，解決了Mw 6.4和Mw 7.1主震之間的級聯互動；在Santorini-Kolumbo案例中，該系統識別出一個結構引導的侵入模型，將斷層通道的間歇性遷移與均勻地殼失效中預期的連續傳播區分開來。通過提供一個可普遍化的邏輯基礎設施來解釋異質地震現象，TRACE將該領域從依賴專家的分析推進到以知識為導向的自主發現，促進地球科學的發展。
2603.21152.author: Feng Liu et.al.
2603.21152.authors: Feng Liu, Jian Xu, Xin Cui, Xinghao Wang, Zijie Guo, Jiong Wang, S. Mostafa Mousavi, Xinyu Gu, Hao Chen, Ben Fei, Lihua Fang, Fenghua Ling, Zefeng Li, Lei Bai
